finding_results.notes: Ina now carries high-contraction pressure speech across all five confrontation turns (We've/isn't/that's/don't/we're/They're/I'm), includes an explicit mid-line corrective pivot ("yes, but no, that's your word for it"), and shifts from abstraction to concrete consequence (wedding ring, rent notices, clinic deadlines, dialysis slot). Kwon remains predominantly formal/uncontracted with statesman-policy diction in his rebuttal.

summary: Revised the confrontation span to restore idiolect separation and pressure cadence: Ina now sounds contracted, corrective, and embodied under stress, while Kwon stays controlled and formal.
